# Probability Disclosure Exposure — Korea (Executive Brief)

## Scope and As-of Date
- Jurisdictions: KR
- As-of date: 2026-05-07
- Assumptions: launch-ready randomized paid item mechanic; Korean-language storefront
- Exclusions: non-randomized monetization, non-Korean markets

## Key Conclusions
1. Probability disclosure for randomized paid items is mandatory under the controlling rule [src_001].
2. Compliance steps must precede commercial launch in Korea [src_001].

## Counter-Analysis and Risk Assessment
Per `knowledge/output-modes/counter-analysis-checklist.md`. No material
alternative interpretation surfaces from the controlling article. Risk
level Low; no `[Material Risk]` flag warranted.

## Risk and Priority Ranking
- High: none
- Medium: enforcement risk if disclosure depth is insufficient
- Low: residual statutory ambiguity

## Practical Implications
- Decision impact: launch must include in-product probability disclosure
- Recommended next step: verify implementing rule depth requirements with counsel

## Immediate Action Checklist
- [ ] Confirm in-product disclosure UI surfaces all randomized item probabilities
- [ ] Retain disclosure logs per record-keeping rules
- [ ] Brief platform partners on Korean-market requirements

## Top Sources
- [src_001] Korean Game Industry Promotion Act probability disclosure provisions — Article 33-2 — paragraphs (1)-(3) — official statute text

## Verification Guide
| Finding | Source | Pinpoint | Verification URL |
|---|---|---|---|
| Disclosure mandatory | [src_001] | Article 33-2(1) | official statute text |
